MOBILECOMMAND.Role.Name: Client role
MOBILECOMMAND.Role.Hint: What this client is at the table. Auto: GM clients act as DM, players as phones. Phase 1 uses this only for executor selection and phone-side warning suppression.
MOBILECOMMAND.ExecutorUser.Name: Executor user (blank = active GM)
MOBILECOMMAND.ExecutorUser.Hint: User id or name of the GM client that executes phone actions and answers spatial queries. Leave blank to use whichever GM is active (the two-client topology). Set this if you later run a dedicated headless Service user.
MOBILECOMMAND.PauseGuard.Name: Auto-pause when executor leaves the active scene
MOBILECOMMAND.PauseGuard.Hint: Phone actions need the executor viewing the active scene. When it navigates away the game pauses; it resumes automatically when the executor returns (manual pauses are never auto-resumed).
MOBILECOMMAND.Heartbeat.Name: Heartbeat interval (seconds)
MOBILECOMMAND.Heartbeat.Hint: How often the executor broadcasts its liveness to phones. 0 disables.
MOBILECOMMAND.SaveTimeout.Name: Expected save prompt timeout (seconds)
MOBILECOMMAND.SaveTimeout.Hint: The midi-qol playerSaveTimeout value the Settings Enforcer polices. Tune to what feels right at the table (DESIGN.md Q4).
MOBILECOMMAND.EnforcerAutoPrompt.Name: Check settings preset on startup
MOBILECOMMAND.EnforcerAutoPrompt.Hint: On world ready, GM clients compare live settings to the canonical preset (DESIGN.md D4) and offer a one-click fix when they drift.
